name: Decompression Hang
description: Decompresses the spine and rehydrates the discs that compress all day — the fastest way to reclaim lost height.
safety_note: Use only a solid wood or solid-core door. Stop if the door creaks, shifts, or shows any sign of stress.

description: Loads the legs and spine to trigger growth hormone and build the strength that holds you upright.

name: Superman Hold
description: Strengthens the entire back chain that fights slouching and keeps the spine extended and tall.
instructions_merged: SUPERMAN HOLD — 2 sets × 30 sec hold | Lie on your stomach with both arms extended forward. | Lift your chest, arms, and legs off the floor at the same time. | Keep your gaze down and hold for the full 30 seconds.

name: Tadasana (Mountain Pose)
description: Trains your body's tallest neutral posture so standing upright becomes your default.
instructions_merged: TADASANA (MOUNTAIN POSE) — 2 sets × 30 sec hold | Stand with your feet together, weight spread evenly. | Engage your thighs, tuck your tailbone, and lift your chest. | Reach the crown of your head upward and hold for the full 30 seconds.

name: Wall Angels
description: Opens the chest and pulls the shoulders back, directly reversing the rounded-shoulder slump.
instructions_merged: WALL ANGELS — 2 sets × 15 reps | Stand with your back flat against a wall, feet 6 inches forward. | Press your arms to the wall in a 'W' shape. | Slide your arms up to a 'Y' and back down for 15 reps, keeping contact with the wall.
